title: French in 60 Days — the Pareto Sprint

days.title: The sounds of French
days.objective: Learn how French actually sounds so every later word sticks correctly.
days.paretoFocus: Pronunciation is the foundation everything rests on. Fix silent endings, nasal vowels and the French 'r' now and you'll never mis-store a word.
drills: Listen and repeat the alphabet and the nasal vowels an/on/in/un.
Read 10 words aloud, dropping the final consonant.
Record yourself saying 'bonjour, merci, au revoir'.

items: Re-listen to today's sounds and shadow them.
Say the 5 nasal-vowel example words from memory.
Write 3 words where the last letter is silent.

days.title: Greetings & politeness
days.objective: Open and close any interaction politely.
days.paretoFocus: A handful of fixed phrases (bonjour, s'il vous plaît, merci, pardon) cover 90% of first impressions and are non-negotiable in French culture.
drills: Memorise: bonjour, bonsoir, salut, au revoir, à bientôt.
Practise s'il vous plaît / merci / de rien / excusez-moi.
Role-play entering a shop and greeting the owner.

items: Self-test: how do you say 'please' formally?

days.title: Introduce yourself
days.objective: Say your name, where you're from, and ask the same.
days.paretoFocus: 'Je m'appelle… / Je viens de… / Et vous ?' lets you start any conversation. High reuse, low effort.
drills: Build your intro: name, nationality, city.
Learn: Comment vous appelez-vous ? / D'où venez-vous ?

items: Give your full intro from memory.
Add 3 nationalities to your Anki deck.

days.title: Noun gender & 'the' / 'a'
days.objective: Understand that every noun is le/la and always learn the article with the word.
days.paretoFocus: Gender errors are the #1 beginner mistake. Learning 'la table' (not 'table') from day one saves months of relearning.
drills: Learn le/la/les and un/une/des with 10 common nouns.
Add each new noun to Anki WITH its article.

items: Quiz: le or la — chat, table, maison, livre?

days.title: Subject pronouns
days.objective: Know je / tu / il / elle / on / nous / vous / ils / elles.
days.paretoFocus: Pronouns are the skeleton of every conjugation table you'll build next week. Memorise them cold now.
drills: Learn when to use tu vs vous.

items: Decide tu/vous for: a friend, a waiter, your boss.

days.title: ÊTRE (to be)
days.objective: Conjugate être in the present and use it to describe yourself.
days.paretoFocus: être is the most common verb in French. With pronouns + être you can already make dozens of sentences.
drills: Memorise: suis, es, est, sommes, êtes, sont.
Make 5 sentences: Je suis…, Il est…, Nous sommes…

days.title: Week 1 review + numbers 0-20
days.objective: Consolidate week 1 and learn to count to 20.
days.paretoFocus: A weekly consolidation locks in gains; numbers unlock prices, time and quantities — pure daily utility.
drills: Re-do the A0 vocabulary in Anki.

days.title: AVOIR (to have)
days.objective: Conjugate avoir and use it for possession, age and hunger.
days.paretoFocus: avoir is the #2 verb AND the engine of the past tense later. 'J'ai 25 ans', 'J'ai faim' are everyday gold.
drills: Memorise: ai, as, a, avons, avez, ont.

days.title: Core vocabulary: people & family
days.objective: Learn 20 high-frequency words for family and people.
days.paretoFocus: Frequency-first vocabulary means every word you learn is one you'll actually use. Family is among the most-discussed topics.
drills: Learn: famille, mère, père, frère, sœur, ami, enfant…
Describe your family with avoir + être.
Add all 20 words to Anki with articles.

days.title: Regular -ER verbs
days.objective: Conjugate the largest verb group: -ER verbs.
days.paretoFocus: ~90% of French verbs are -ER and conjugate identically. Learn ONE pattern and unlock thousands of verbs.

days.title: Negation: ne … pas
days.objective: Make any sentence negative.
days.paretoFocus: One simple wrap (ne…pas) negates everything. Doubles your expressive range instantly.
drills: Practise n' before vowels: Je n'aime pas.

days.title: Asking yes/no questions
days.objective: Ask questions with intonation and est-ce que.
days.paretoFocus: Two easy question forms let you start gathering information — the heart of real conversation.
drills: Ask a partner/AI 3 questions about themselves.

items: Ask 'Do you speak English?' two ways.

days.title: Question words
days.objective: Use qui, que, où, quand, pourquoi, comment, combien.
days.paretoFocus: Seven question words open every information-seeking sentence you'll ever need.

days.title: A0 milestone check
days.objective: Prove you can greet, introduce yourself and order a coffee in French.
days.paretoFocus: Milestone checks convert scattered knowledge into real-world capability and reveal exactly what to review.
drills: Take the A0 quiz in the app.

items: Complete the A0 Feynman: 'How French gender works'.

days.title: ALLER (to go) + places
days.objective: Conjugate aller and talk about going places.
days.paretoFocus: aller is verb #3 and the key to the easy future tense you'll learn next. Also covers directions and travel.
drills: Memorise: vais, vas, va, allons, allez, vont.
Use à + place: Je vais à Paris.
Learn à la / au / aux contractions.

days.title: The easy future: aller + infinitive
days.objective: Talk about plans without learning a new tense.
days.paretoFocus: Futur proche reuses aller (already known) + any infinitive. Massive expressive gain for near-zero cost.
drills: Ask: Qu'est-ce que tu vas faire ?

days.title: FAIRE (to do/make)
days.objective: Conjugate faire and use it in set expressions.
days.paretoFocus: faire is verb #4 and hides in countless idioms (faire les courses, faire attention, il fait beau). High coverage.
drills: Memorise: fais, fais, fait, faisons, faites, font.
Learn weather: Il fait beau / froid.

days.title: Food & ordering
days.objective: Order food and drink and ask the price.
days.paretoFocus: Restaurants/cafés are where you'll use French first. 'Je voudrais…' + 'L'addition' covers the whole interaction.
drills: Learn: je voudrais, l'addition, sur place / à emporter.

days.title: Numbers 20-100 & time
days.objective: Handle prices, phone numbers and the clock.
days.paretoFocus: Numbers and time are pure utility — needed for shopping, appointments and travel every single day.
drills: Tell the time: Il est trois heures.

days.title: Adjectives & agreement
days.objective: Describe things with adjectives that agree in gender and number.
days.paretoFocus: Adjective agreement is everywhere; getting the pattern (add -e for fem, -s for plural) early prevents fossilised errors.
drills: Note position: usually AFTER the noun (un livre rouge).

days.title: Week 3 review + free talk
days.objective: Consolidate the 4 power verbs and futur proche in conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Active recall in conversation is where knowledge becomes skill. Weekly free-talk forces retrieval.
drills: 3-minute monologue about your week and plans.

items: Describe a plan with futur proche.
List any words you blanked on for tomorrow.

days.title: Prepositions & places in town
days.objective: Say where things are and give simple directions.
days.paretoFocus: A dozen prepositions (à, de, dans, sur, sous, à côté de…) describe nearly all spatial relationships.

items: Say 'The cat is under the table'.

days.title: Possessives (my, your, his/her)
days.objective: Express possession with mon/ma/mes, ton/ta/tes, son/sa/ses.
days.paretoFocus: Possessives are constant in speech; the agreement-with-the-noun quirk is worth drilling now.
drills: Note: agreement is with the object, not the owner.

items: Say 'my mother, my father, my friends'.

days.title: Daily routine & reflexive verbs
days.objective: Describe your day with se lever, se laver, se coucher.
days.paretoFocus: Reflexive verbs cover the entire daily routine — one of the most practised speaking topics at A1.
drills: Add time markers: d'abord, ensuite, enfin.

items: Tell the time for 3 routine moments.

days.title: Shopping & quantities
days.objective: Buy things and talk about how much/many.
days.paretoFocus: Partitives (du/de la/des) and quantity words are needed every time you shop or cook.
drills: Learn du / de la / des and beaucoup de / un peu de.

items: Ask for 'a bit of bread' and 'some water'.

days.title: Connectors & longer sentences
days.objective: Join ideas with et, mais, parce que, donc, alors.
days.paretoFocus: Connectors turn isolated phrases into real discourse — the jump from words to conversation.

items: Give a reason for something you like.

days.title: Listening day (comprehensible input)
days.objective: Train your ear with slow, clear French.
days.paretoFocus: Listening is half of communication and the most-neglected skill. Regular input accelerates everything else.

items: Summarise what you understood in English.

days.title: Introducing the past (passé composé)
days.objective: Form the everyday past tense with avoir + participle.
days.paretoFocus: The passé composé is THE past tense of speech. Once you have it, you can tell stories about anything.

items: Say 'I ate / I spoke / I worked' in the past.

days.title: Passé composé with être
days.objective: Use être for movement verbs and make the participle agree.
days.paretoFocus: A small fixed set of verbs (aller, venir, partir…) takes être — memorise the list once and you're covered.
drills: Say 'I went / she came / they left'.
Make the participle agree with elle/ils.

days.title: Phase 1 milestone: a real conversation
days.objective: Hold a simple but real conversation about yourself, past and plans.
days.paretoFocus: Integrating present, past and future in live speech is the true A1 milestone — proof the core works together.
drills: Cover: who you are, yesterday, tomorrow.

items: Take the A1 quiz in the app.

days.title: -IR and -RE verbs
days.objective: Conjugate the second and third verb groups in the present.
days.paretoFocus: After -ER, the -IR and -RE patterns cover almost all remaining common verbs (finir, choisir, vendre, attendre).
drills: Mix all three groups in 6 sentences.

days.title: Common irregular verbs I
days.objective: Learn vouloir, pouvoir, devoir (want/can/must).
days.paretoFocus: These three modal-like verbs + an infinitive express desire, ability and obligation — enormous everyday range.
drills: Learn je veux / je peux / je dois.

items: Say 'I want to / I can / I must…'.

days.title: Common irregular verbs II
days.objective: Learn prendre, venir, voir, savoir.
days.paretoFocus: These high-frequency irregulars appear constantly; prendre also covers transport and food.
drills: Use prendre for transport and meals.

items: Say 'I take the train / I come from…'.

days.title: Object pronouns (le, la, les, lui, leur)
days.objective: Replace nouns with pronouns to avoid repetition.
days.paretoFocus: Pronouns make speech natural and concise; even partial mastery instantly raises your fluency.
drills: Learn direct le/la/les and indirect lui/leur.
Note pronoun position before the verb.

items: Replace 'I see the cat' with a pronoun.

days.title: Describing past habits (imparfait intro)
days.objective: Use the imparfait for 'used to' and background description.
days.paretoFocus: Knowing when to use imparfait vs passé composé is the key past-tense distinction — start the contrast now.
drills: Describe what you used to do as a child.
Contrast one imparfait vs passé composé pair.

items: Say 'When I was young, I played…'.
Note the passé composé vs imparfait rule.

days.title: Listening + dictation
days.objective: Sharpen comprehension and spelling through dictation.
days.paretoFocus: Dictation forces you to hear word boundaries and silent endings — fixing both listening and writing at once.

days.title: Week review + 5-minute monologue
days.objective: Speak for 5 minutes mixing all tenses.
days.paretoFocus: Longer monologues build the stamina and automaticity that flashcards can't.

days.title: Comparatives & superlatives
days.objective: Compare things: plus … que, moins … que, le plus.
days.paretoFocus: Comparisons are everywhere in opinions and shopping; the plus/moins pattern is simple and high-yield.

days.title: Talking about the weather & seasons
days.objective: Discuss weather, seasons and clothing.
days.paretoFocus: Weather is a universal small-talk opener and recycles il fait + adjectives you already know.

days.title: Health & the body
days.objective: Explain how you feel and basic symptoms.
days.paretoFocus: 'J'ai mal à…' is essential survival language for travel and reuses avoir + body parts.

days.title: Travel & transport
days.objective: Buy tickets and navigate transport.
days.paretoFocus: Transport vocabulary + prendre + directions cover the entire travel experience that motivates many learners.
drills: Learn transport vocab and 'un billet pour…'.

items: Buy a return ticket to Lyon.

days.title: Future simple (intro)
days.objective: Recognise and form the simple future for common verbs.
days.paretoFocus: While futur proche covers speech, recognising the futur simple unlocks reading and more precise plans.
drills: Form the future of 5 regular verbs.

items: Contrast futur proche vs futur simple.

days.title: Reading day
days.objective: Read a short graded text and mine it for language.
days.paretoFocus: Reading builds vocabulary in context and reinforces grammar passively — efficient, low-stress input.
drills: Read a short A1/A2 text twice.

days.title: Week review + conversation
days.objective: Use travel, health and weather in a live conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Theme-mixing conversation simulates real life, where topics don't come pre-sorted.

days.title: Expressing opinions
days.objective: Give and justify opinions: je pense que, à mon avis.
days.paretoFocus: Opinion frames let you say something about anything — the engine of all interesting conversation.

days.title: The conditional (politeness & wishes)
days.objective: Use je voudrais, j'aimerais, pourriez-vous.
days.paretoFocus: The conditional makes you sound polite and natural; just a few forms transform your register.

items: Note conditional = future stem + imparfait ending.

days.title: Storytelling: combining past tenses
days.objective: Tell a short story mixing passé composé and imparfait.
days.paretoFocus: Storytelling is the ultimate integrative skill and the clearest sign you've internalised the past tenses.
drills: Set the scene in imparfait, events in passé composé.

items: Note one tense error to fix.

days.title: Phone & email basics
days.objective: Handle a simple phone call and write a short message.
days.paretoFocus: Set phrases for calls/emails (Allô, je vous appelle pour…, Cordialement) are formulaic and high-utility.

days.title: Listening sprint
days.objective: Stretch comprehension with slightly faster input.
days.paretoFocus: Gradually increasing audio speed trains real-world listening, the skill learners most often lack.
drills: Listen to an episode at normal speed.

days.title: Week review + mock interview
days.objective: Answer common questions about yourself fluently.
days.paretoFocus: Rehearsing high-frequency questions builds the automatic responses that real conversations demand.
drills: Answer 10 common questions about your life.

days.title: Vocabulary consolidation
days.objective: Lock in your most useful 600 words.
days.paretoFocus: Reviewing the high-frequency core ensures the 20% of words that do 80% of the work are fully automatic.
drills: Do a full Anki review session.
Use 10 review words in fresh sentences.

items: Test yourself on 20 random core words.

days.title: Culture & register (tu/vous, politeness)
days.objective: Use the right level of formality in different settings.
days.paretoFocus: Register mistakes feel rude; knowing when to use tu/vous and set politeness formulas is socially essential.
drills: Re-do a role-play formally then informally.

days.title: Common mistakes clinic
days.objective: Target the errors you personally keep making.
days.paretoFocus: Fixing your top 5 recurring mistakes yields more improvement than learning new material.

items: Update Anki with personal error cards.

days.title: Free conversation day
days.objective: Have an unscripted 15-minute conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Unscripted speaking is the real test; it builds the spontaneity that defines functional A1+.
drills: Push to use past, present and future.

items: Look up the words you lacked.

days.title: Reading a real text
days.objective: Read an authentic short article or menu/blog.
days.paretoFocus: Authentic material proves your skills transfer beyond textbooks and exposes real usage.

days.title: Writing day
days.objective: Write a 150-word text about your life and plans.
days.paretoFocus: Writing forces precision in grammar and spelling and consolidates everything you've learned.

days.title: Full cheatsheet review
days.objective: Re-derive the entire one-page summary from memory.
days.paretoFocus: Being able to reproduce the core map proves the 20% is internalised, not just recognised.
drills: Cover the cheatsheet; rebuild each section.
Conjugate all 4 power verbs + one of each group.
Explain gender, negation and questions aloud.

items: Check your reconstruction against the app.

days.title: Feynman day: teach it all back
days.objective: Explain French's core systems as if teaching a friend.
days.paretoFocus: Teaching is the strongest test of understanding; gaps surface immediately and get fixed.
drills: Complete all French Feynman concepts in the app.
Re-teach any you can't explain cleanly.

items: Review the cheatsheet gaps from yesterday.

days.title: Mock A1 exam
days.objective: Simulate a DELF A1-style test across all four skills.
days.paretoFocus: A timed, four-skill mock reveals exactly where you stand and what to prioritise next.
drills: Do listening, reading, writing and a speaking task.
Take both A0 and A1 quizzes in the app.

days.title: Milestone: solid A1 + your A2 roadmap
days.objective: Confirm A1 mastery and plan the path to A2.
days.paretoFocus: Closing the sprint with a clear next plan turns a 60-day burst into a lasting habit.
drills: Write your A2 study plan (imparfait, pronouns, more vocab).

items: Tick off every A1 can-do statement.
Schedule daily Anki + weekly tutor going forward.
Celebrate — you reached A1 in 60 days!
